title: Verifiable Privacy for Cloud AI
modules: What Cloud AI Deployments Actually Do to Your Data
Confidential Computing and Trusted Execution Environments Explained
Tool Lab: Auditing Privacy Controls in Major Cloud AI Platforms
Privacy-Preserving Techniques: Federated Learning, Differential Privacy, and Homomorphic Encryption
Building a Verifiable Privacy Case for Regulators and Auditors
synopsis: Sending sensitive data to cloud-hosted AI models without verifiable privacy guarantees exposes organizations to data misuse, regulatory breach, and supply-chain trust failures they cannot detect until damage is done. This course teaches professionals how confidential computing, federated learning, and cryptographic privacy techniques make AI processing verifiable — not just promised — and how to audit and document those guarantees.
rationale: GDPR, HIPAA, and emerging AI-specific regulations are increasingly demanding demonstrable privacy guarantees for AI workloads, yet most security teams lack the technical vocabulary or tooling knowledge to assess or enforce them.
learning_outcome: Learners can audit a cloud AI deployment for verifiable privacy controls, identify gaps between vendor privacy claims and cryptographic guarantees, and produce compliance-ready documentation of their findings.
source_signals: Launch HN: Tinfoil (YC X25): Verifiable Privacy for Cloud AI
nearest_existing: How AI Systems Consume and Expose Personal Data
